content: Hey, I'm so frustrated with the pollution in Pittsburgh. I just moved here from the Bay Area and it's like a whole different world.
I can understand why you'd feel that way. Pittsburgh has a long history of industrial pollution. What specifically is bothering you the most about the pollution here?

Well, I work from home as a Fashion Buyer, so I don't have to commute too much, but when I do go out, I notice the air quality is really bad. And I love DJing, but all the events I play at have so much waste.

That's really interesting. It's great that you're aware of the impact of your work and hobbies on the environment. Have you considered getting involved in any local environmental initiatives or groups?
Actually, I have been looking into some groups that organize clean-ups along the Allegheny River. I've also been trying to reduce my own waste by making origami animals out of recycled paper.
That sounds like a fun and creative way to reuse materials. I've heard that the Allegheny River clean-ups can be really impactful. Do you have a favorite spot along the river that you like to visit?

Yeah, I love going to Point State Park. It's so beautiful, especially during the summer when they have the fireworks displays. By the way, I'm planning a trip to New York City on August 15th to visit some sustainable fashion brands.
That sounds like a great trip! New York City has a lot of innovative fashion brands that are focused on sustainability. Which brands are you most excited to visit?
I'm really looking forward to checking out Patagonia and Reformation. I've heard great things about their environmentally-friendly practices.

Both of those brands are leaders in sustainable fashion. You might also want to check out some of the local thrift stores and vintage shops in NYC. They can be a great way to find unique, eco-friendly pieces.
That's a great idea. I love thrift shopping. And speaking of unique pieces, I've been trying to get into speedcubing as a way to relax. Have you ever heard of it?
Yes, I'm familiar with speedcubing. It's a really interesting hobby that requires a lot of focus and hand-eye coordination. Do you have a favorite cube or technique that you like to use?
I'm still a beginner, but I love using my Moyu RS3M cube. It's so smooth and fast.
That's a great cube for beginners. Maybe one day you can solve it in under a minute!
Ha! I doubt that, but it's fun to try. Anyway, thanks for chatting with me about environmental concerns. It's been really helpful to talk through some of my thoughts and ideas.
You're welcome, Morgan. It was great chatting with you too. Remember to keep reducing, reusing, and recycling, and don't hesitate to reach out if you have any more questions or concerns.
